1_answer_correctness.justification: Ground truth is fully deterministic: each test input has an expected output string in the dataset, and evaluate_reply scores via exact match (reply.answers[k] == v) over all 487 test inputs. Answers are unambiguous and verifiable in code.

2_construct_validity.justification: The task directly operationalizes the claimed capability (induce simple letter mappings from observations, verify, and adapt when later observations override earlier ones) using a stateful multi-turn chat with explicit override semantics in the data, tightly matching the 'learn/verify/adapt loop' claim; output-format confounds are mitigated with a strict YAML spec and parse-failure handling.

3_code_quality.justification: Functional with clean dataclasses (LearningTask, ModelReply), fenced-YAML extraction, type validation, and try/except handling for unparseable replies with a fallback empty reply; but it has rough edges: hardcoded /kaggle/input path, all 130 tasks run in a single conversation history (context-drift risk), no markdown, and a bare-ish broad 'except Exception'.

4_sample_size.justification: 130 sequential tasks comprising 487 individual test inputs, far exceeding the >20 threshold and enabling reliable aggregate scoring.

5_metric_design.justification: Effectively a single accuracy dimension (correct_answers / total_test_inputs) multiplied by penalty factors for an intro response and for any unparseable replies; no second performance dimension (no calibration, latency, or per-condition breakdown) and no markdown justification of metric choice.

6_problem_statement.justification: The benchmark_task_description names the target capability (reason out rules from observations, verify, adapt to contradictions, repeat across interactions) and gives basic motivation, but does not connect it to a broader research question or identify a gap relative to existing benchmarks.

7_technical_documentation.justification: The notebook is a single code cell with zero markdown; there is no documentation of dataset provenance, the override/schema design, scoring rules, or metric interpretation. A reader must reverse-engineer the YAML format and evaluation from the code.

8_capability_targeting.justification: Targets a specific sub-capability (incremental rule induction with adaptation to overriding observations) with an implicit hypothesis, but the code includes no explicit experimental controls, difficulty tiers, or override-vs-non-override condition labels, so performance cannot be cleanly attributed to the adaptation mechanism specifically.

9_item_diversity.justification: Items vary naturally in observation count (2-22) and complexity and include override dynamics across tasks, but there is no labeled taxonomy or difficulty stratification and the notebook reports only an aggregate score, with no per-condition breakdown to reveal where models fail.

10_model_coverage.justification: Only a single .run(kbench.llm, tasks) invocation with one model's results (result=0.108); no cross-model comparison is present.

1_answer_correctness.justification: The benchmark folder is empty: no notebook, dataset, or source note exists. No ground truth or verification logic is defined anywhere.

2_construct_validity.justification: No task page text or notebook implementation is present, so there is nothing to align with the implied 'executive functions / cognitive control' claim; construct validity cannot be established.

3_code_quality.justification: No notebook or code exists for the task; per the rubric, absence of a task notebook is Level 0.

4_sample_size.justification: No dataset or evaluation items are present, yielding effectively zero items, far below the 10-item threshold.

5_metric_design.justification: No metrics are computed because no notebook or evaluation logic exists.

6_problem_statement.justification: No task page or description is provided beyond the folder name, so a reader cannot understand what the task measures or why it exists.

7_technical_documentation.justification: No documentation of dataset provenance, methodology, or scoring exists; the folder contains no files at all.

8_capability_targeting.justification: With no materials present, there is no defined capability, hypothesis, or experimental control to evaluate.

9_item_diversity.justification: No items exist, so there is no taxonomy, sub-condition labeling, or difficulty stratification.

10_model_coverage.justification: No notebook or run results exist, so the task has been run on zero models.
